mnemonics: HASH: Have A Scalable Hash-table. PRIORITY: Put Really Important Or Urgent Tasks In Yours.
Dynamic Hashing: Don't Yell, 'My Nodes Are Full!' (It grows dynamically). Priority Queues: Your Urgent ERrand Is Top (items are dequeued by priority, not order).
keyPoints: Dynamic Hashing allows a hash table to grow by dynamically adding buckets, preventing performance degradation as data grows.
A Priority Queue is an abstract data type where each element has a 'priority'. The element with the highest priority is dequeued first, not the one added first (FIFO).
Heaps (especially Binary Heaps) are the most common data structure used to efficiently implement a Priority Queue, enabling O(log n) insertions and deletions.
commonMistakes: Confusing a Priority Queue with a standard FIFO Queue. The order of removal is based on priority, not insertion time.
Assuming Dynamic Hashing simply means 'separate chaining'. It specifically refers to techniques like Extendible or Linear Hashing that add new buckets on the fly.
